title: glandularia incisa
intro: Glandularia incisa, also known as Rockrose or Penstemon, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its distinctive white, pink, or purple flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Glandularia incisa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Glandularia incisa can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in salves and ointments, making it a versatile and valuable herbal remedy.
